title: stipa coronata var. parishii
intro: Stipa coronata var. parishii, also known as Parish's Needlegrass, is a type of grass native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Stipa coronata var. parishii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
